[[平安時代]]には[[熊野街道]]にあったため[[熊野詣]]の人々が参詣して旅の安全を祈願した。
[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に向泉寺が兵火で焼失し、後に現・堺区市之町東に再建された。
[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）には[[東京]]遷都の折に17日間の祈祷の命を蒙った。また、[[神仏分離]]によって神宮寺の向泉寺は廃寺とされた。
[[1873年]]（明治6年）3月、[[郷社]]に列している。[[1907年]]（明治40年）2月に[[水天宮]]社を合祀し、同年10月には当社の南東にあり[[泉北郡]][[向井町|向井村]]の氏神社である向井神社（[[牛頭天王]]社）とその境内社（[[愛宕神社]]、[[神明社]]、小祠四社）を合祀して方違天王神社と改称するが、翌11月に方違神社に復号した。[[1908年]]（明治41年）12月、[[八幡社]]と武内社を合祀する。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）7月10日の堺大空襲（第6回[[大阪大空襲]]）で社殿が焼失するが、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に再建した。[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月に社殿が建て替えられた<ref>ザ・古墳群 p.104</ref>。
現在でも、転勤、結婚などでの転宅や海外旅行などの際に祈願する参拝者が多い。自分の在所から出かけていく先の方位についてのお祓いをしてもらい、清めの御砂を頂いて、自分の家の四方に撒く。
古くから方除けには当地の埴土を包んだ[[ちまき]]を奉ったことから、厄除守に御札のほか御砂、ちまきがある。
境内には三国丘碑、養蚕興業碑などがある。当社は[[反正天皇]]陵の北東部に接している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 方違幸大神［八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）］ * 合祀神 - 向井大神（大鷦鷯天皇（[[仁徳天皇]]）、去来穂別天皇（[[履中天皇]]）、瑞歯別天皇（[[反正天皇]]）、[[菟道稚郎子]]命、[[百済]][[王仁]]）、[[水天宮]]外十二柱神
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月再建。 * 幣殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 拝殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 神庫 * 祭具庫 * 社務所 * 楠木姫大神 * 大年大明神 * 鈴山大明神 * 白髪大明神 * 末次大明神 * [[八幡大神]] * [[神明社]] * 神功皇后御馬繁之松 <gallery> Hochigai-jinja haiden.jpg|方違神社の旧拝殿 2007年7月15日撮影 Houchigai jinja honden.jpg|新拝殿の斜めショット Mikunigaoka sekihi 01.jpg|三国丘石碑 Mikunigaoka sekihi 02.jpg|平木林作石碑 </gallery>
== 文化財== === 大阪府指定天然記念物 === * [[クロガネモチ|くろがねもち]] - 境内ではなく、当社の南東側、通称「けやき通り」の歩道内にある。この地はかつて向井神社の境内であった。胸高径1.1m、幹周3.5m、樹高6.8m（[[1992年]]時点）。[[1973年]]（昭和48年）3月30日、大阪府から[[天然記念物]]に指定された。[[2018年]]現在は樹勢が衰えており、鉄柱によって支えられている<ref>[http://www.tree-flower.jp/27/hochigai_jinja/kuroganemochi.htm 方違神社のクロガネモチ]</ref>。
=== 堺市指定保存樹木 === * [[クスノキ]] - 指定番号8、指定年月日：[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月15日<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/hozen/kojumeiboku.html 堺市保存樹木・樹林一覧]</ref>。 * [[イチョウ]] - 指定番号9、指定年月日：1980年（昭和55年）3月15日。 <gallery> Kuroganemochi Hochigai Shrine,Sakai.jpg|けやき通りのくろがねもち Preserved tree, houchigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキ Sign of preserved tree, houtigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識1 Sign preserved tree, houtigai shine2.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識2 </gallery>
== 祭事・行事 == 以下の祭事・行事がある。 * 粽祭（例大祭）（5月31日） - 粽（ちまき）を供える祭り<ref>神山幸雄「方違神社」（大阪春秋社『大阪春秋』第9巻第3号、p.87、1981年）</ref>。方違神社は摂津・河内・和泉のどこにも属さない場所にあるという考え方から、境内の土で作られた粽は悪い方位を祓うとされる<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.171、2008年</ref>。神功皇后が祓をしたという故事に由来する<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.192、2008年</ref>。当日は13時から神事が行われる<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://hochigai-jinja.or.jp/5gatu.html |title=方違神社 粽祭り |publisher =方違神社|accessdate =2018-07-14}}</ref>。 * 初午祭（8月6日） * 月次祭（毎月1日・15日）
== 故事== [[神武天皇]]の東征時、その兄が反旗を翻した。神武天皇の夢まくらに老人が立ち、瓦を作り、飴を作って神前に備えるとどんな逆賊も平定できるとのお告げを得て、兄の一族を降参させた。その後、神后皇后が堺の浦に上陸し、三国丘に上陸した際、葦の葉に当地の埴土を包んで方災の祓をし、埴土で飴を作って神前に備え、治国平天下を祈願した。
二月の節分に飴を売る店が出て、一家が祝って食べる習慣が残る。また、方違神社の紋が[[ちまき]]であるのは、葦の葉に埴土を包んで方災よけをされた神后皇后の故事により、5月31日の例祭には[[ちまき]]を供える<ref>伝説の堺 p.40−42</ref>。
== 交通機関 == * [[南海電気鉄道|南海]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から東へ徒歩5分 * [[阪和線|JR阪和線]][[堺市駅]]より西へ徒歩10分 * [[南海バス]]方違神社前 下車すぐ * [[阪神高速15号堺線]] 堺出口より車で5分
== 出典 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|publisher=[[大阪府教育委員会]]文化財保護課 |title=大阪府の御供物行事 |date=2008-3 |editor=大阪府伝統文化総合支援研究委員会HBネットワーキング |series=文化庁「ふるさと文化再興事業」大阪府伝統文化総合支援研究委嘱事業実施報告書 |id={{全国書誌番号|21479121}} }} * 神山幸雄「方違神社」（大阪春秋社『大阪春秋』第9巻第3号）、1981年 * {{Citation|和書|title=ザ・古墳群 : 百舌鳥と古市全89基 |publisher=140B |date=2018-5 |isbn=978-4-903993-34-8 }} * {{Citation|和書|author=松本荘吉 |title=伝説の堺 |publisher=歴史図書社 |date=1978-7 |id={{全国書誌番号|78025189}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[方位神]] * [[大将軍神社]] * [[神社一覧]] * [[ふとん太鼓]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Hochigai-jinja}} * {{Official website}}

{{神社 | 名称=方違神社 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1 | 位置={{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神= 方違幸大神（八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気足長姫命（[[神功皇后]]）） | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年） | 本殿= | 例祭 = 5月31日 | 画像=[[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=方違神社|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''方違神社'''（ほうちがいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]にある[[神社]]。地元では「ほうちがいさん」と称されている。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。祭神は方違幸大神（かたたがえさちおおかみ）。古くから[[方違え]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 社伝では、[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年）に、勅願により物部大母呂隅足尼（もののべのおおもろすみのすくね）を茅渟の石津原に遣わせて[[スサノオ|須佐之男神]]を祀らせたとする。これが当社創建の起源という。
その後、[[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]終了後に[[忍熊皇子|忍熊王]]の叛乱に遭った際、当地に[[天神地祇]]を祀って方災除けを祈願し、合戦に勝利している。更に[[応神天皇]]が須佐之男神と天神地祇の他に三筒男神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）と神功皇后を祀り、方違大依羅神社（かたたがへおおよさみのかむつやしろ）と号し、この社を方違宮と称したとされる。
社地は[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境の三国山（現・三国ヶ丘）にあり、三[[令制国]]のいずれにも属さない地、方位のない地であるとして、古くから[[方位]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として有名であった。
[[奈良時代]]には[[行基]]が伏屋や井戸を設けて人馬往来の要衝として栄えた。[[天平]]15年（[[743年]]）には後に[[神宮寺]]となる向泉寺（現・[[大阪府立三国丘高等学校|三国丘高校]]の地）が建立されている。
[[平安時代]]には[[熊野街道]]にあったため[[熊野詣]]の人々が参詣して旅の安全を祈願した。
[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に向泉寺が兵火で焼失し、後に現・堺区市之町東に再建された。
[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）には[[東京]]遷都の折に17日間の祈祷の命を蒙った。また、[[神仏分離]]によって神宮寺の向泉寺は廃寺とされた。
[[1873年]]（明治6年）3月、[[郷社]]に列している。[[1907年]]（明治40年）2月に[[水天宮]]社を合祀し、同年10月には当社の南東にあり[[泉北郡]][[向井町|向井村]]の氏神社である向井神社（[[牛頭天王]]社）とその境内社（[[愛宕神社]]、[[神明社]]、小祠四社）を合祀して方違天王神社と改称するが、翌11月に方違神社に復号した。[[1908年]]（明治41年）12月、[[八幡社]]と武内社を合祀する。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）7月10日の堺大空襲（第6回[[大阪大空襲]]）で社殿が焼失するが、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に再建した。[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月に社殿が建て替えられた<ref>ザ・古墳群 p.104</ref>。
現在でも、転勤、結婚などでの転宅や海外旅行などの際に祈願する参拝者が多い。自分の在所から出かけていく先の方位についてのお祓いをしてもらい、清めの御砂を頂いて、自分の家の四方に撒く。
古くから方除けには当地の埴土を包んだ[[ちまき]]を奉ったことから、厄除守に御札のほか御砂、ちまきがある。
境内には三国丘碑、養蚕興業碑などがある。当社は[[反正天皇]]陵の北東部に接している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 方違幸大神［八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）］ * 合祀神 - 向井大神（大鷦鷯天皇（[[仁徳天皇]]）、去来穂別天皇（[[履中天皇]]）、瑞歯別天皇（[[反正天皇]]）、[[菟道稚郎子]]命、[[百済]][[王仁]]）、[[水天宮]]外十二柱神
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月再建。 * 幣殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 拝殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 神庫 * 祭具庫 * 社務所 * 楠木姫大神 * 大年大明神 * 鈴山大明神 * 白髪大明神 * 末次大明神 * [[八幡大神]] * [[神明社]] * 神功皇后御馬繁之松 <gallery> Hochigai-jinja haiden.jpg|方違神社の旧拝殿 2007年7月15日撮影 Houchigai jinja honden.jpg|新拝殿の斜めショット Mikunigaoka sekihi 01.jpg|三国丘石碑 Mikunigaoka sekihi 02.jpg|平木林作石碑 </gallery>
== 文化財== === 大阪府指定天然記念物 === * [[クロガネモチ|くろがねもち]] - 境内ではなく、当社の南東側、通称「けやき通り」の歩道内にある。この地はかつて向井神社の境内であった。胸高径1.1m、幹周3.5m、樹高6.8m（[[1992年]]時点）。[[1973年]]（昭和48年）3月30日、大阪府から[[天然記念物]]に指定された。[[2018年]]現在は樹勢が衰えており、鉄柱によって支えられている<ref>[http://www.tree-flower.jp/27/hochigai_jinja/kuroganemochi.htm 方違神社のクロガネモチ]</ref>。
=== 堺市指定保存樹木 === * [[クスノキ]] - 指定番号8、指定年月日：[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月15日<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/hozen/kojumeiboku.html 堺市保存樹木・樹林一覧]</ref>。 * [[イチョウ]] - 指定番号9、指定年月日：1980年（昭和55年）3月15日。 <gallery> Kuroganemochi Hochigai Shrine,Sakai.jpg|けやき通りのくろがねもち Preserved tree, houchigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキ Sign of preserved tree, houtigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識1 Sign preserved tree, houtigai shine2.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識2 </gallery>

== 故事== [[神武天皇]]の東征時、その兄が反旗を翻した。神武天皇の夢まくらに老人が立ち、瓦を作り、飴を作って神前に備えるとどんな逆賊も平定できるとのお告げを得て、兄の一族を降参させた。その後、神后皇后が堺の浦に上陸し、三国丘に上陸した際、葦の葉に当地の埴土を包んで方災の祓をし、埴土で飴を作って神前に備え、治国平天下を祈願した。
二月の節分に飴を売る店が出て、一家が祝って食べる習慣が残る。また、方違神社の紋が[[ちまき]]であるのは、葦の葉に埴土を包んで方災よけをされた神后皇后の故事により、5月31日の例祭には[[ちまき]]を供える<ref>伝説の堺 p.40−42</ref>。

{{神社 | 名称=方違神社 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1 | 位置={{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神= 方違幸大神（八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気足長姫命（[[神功皇后]]）） | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年） | 本殿= | 例祭 = 5月31日 | 画像=[[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=方違神社|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''方違神社'''（ほうちがいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]にある[[神社]]。地元では「ほうちがいさん」と称されている。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。祭神は方違幸大神（かたたがえさちおおかみ）。古くから[[方違え]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 社伝では、[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年）に、勅願により物部大母呂隅足尼（もののべのおおもろすみのすくね）を茅渟の石津原に遣わせて[[スサノオ|須佐之男神]]を祀らせたとする。これが当社創建の起源という。
その後、[[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]終了後に[[忍熊皇子|忍熊王]]の叛乱に遭った際、当地に[[天神地祇]]を祀って方災除けを祈願し、合戦に勝利している。更に[[応神天皇]]が須佐之男神と天神地祇の他に三筒男神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）と神功皇后を祀り、方違大依羅神社（かたたがへおおよさみのかむつやしろ）と号し、この社を方違宮と称したとされる。
社地は[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境の三国山（現・三国ヶ丘）にあり、三[[令制国]]のいずれにも属さない地、方位のない地であるとして、古くから[[方位]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として有名であった。
[[奈良時代]]には[[行基]]が伏屋や井戸を設けて人馬往来の要衝として栄えた。[[天平]]15年（[[743年]]）には後に[[神宮寺]]となる向泉寺（現・[[大阪府立三国丘高等学校|三国丘高校]]の地）が建立されている。
[[平安時代]]には[[熊野街道]]にあったため[[熊野詣]]の人々が参詣して旅の安全を祈願した。
[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に向泉寺が兵火で焼失し、後に現・堺区市之町東に再建された。
[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）には[[東京奠都]]の折に17日間の祈祷の命を蒙った。また、[[神仏分離]]によって神宮寺の向泉寺は廃寺とされた。
[[1873年]]（明治6年）3月、[[郷社]]に列している。[[1907年]]（明治40年）2月に[[水天宮]]社を合祀し、同年10月には当社の南東にあり[[泉北郡]][[向井町|向井村]]の氏神社である向井神社（[[牛頭天王]]社）とその境内社（[[愛宕神社]]、[[神明社]]、小祠四社）を合祀して方違天王神社と改称するが、翌11月に方違神社に復号した。[[1908年]]（明治41年）12月、[[八幡社]]と武内社を合祀する。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）7月10日の堺大空襲（第6回[[大阪大空襲]]）で社殿が焼失するが、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に再建した。[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月に社殿が建て替えられた<ref>ザ・古墳群 p.104</ref>。
現在でも、転勤、結婚などでの転宅や海外旅行などの際に祈願する参拝者が多い。自分の在所から出かけていく先の方位についてのお祓いをしてもらい、清めの御砂を頂いて、自分の家の四方に撒く。
古くから方除けには当地の埴土を包んだ[[ちまき]]を奉ったことから、厄除守に御札のほか御砂、ちまきがある。
境内には三国丘碑、養蚕興業碑などがある。当社は[[反正天皇]]陵の北東部に接している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 方違幸大神［八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）］ * 合祀神 - 向井大神（大鷦鷯天皇（[[仁徳天皇]]）、去来穂別天皇（[[履中天皇]]）、瑞歯別天皇（[[反正天皇]]）、[[菟道稚郎子]]命、[[百済]][[王仁]]）、[[水天宮]]外十二柱神
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月再建。 * 幣殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 拝殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 神庫 * 祭具庫 * 社務所 * 楠木姫大神 * 大年大明神 * 鈴山大明神 * 白髪大明神 * 末次大明神 * [[八幡大神]] * [[神明社]] * 神功皇后御馬繁之松 <gallery> Hochigai-jinja haiden.jpg|方違神社の旧拝殿 2007年7月15日撮影 Houchigai jinja honden.jpg|新拝殿の斜めショット Mikunigaoka sekihi 01.jpg|三国丘石碑 Mikunigaoka sekihi 02.jpg|平木林作石碑 </gallery>
== 文化財== === 大阪府指定天然記念物 === * [[クロガネモチ|くろがねもち]] - 境内ではなく、当社の南東側、通称「けやき通り」の歩道内にある。この地はかつて向井神社の境内であった。胸高径1.1m、幹周3.5m、樹高6.8m（[[1992年]]時点）。[[1973年]]（昭和48年）3月30日、大阪府から[[天然記念物]]に指定された。[[2018年]]現在は樹勢が衰えており、鉄柱によって支えられている<ref>[http://www.tree-flower.jp/27/hochigai_jinja/kuroganemochi.htm 方違神社のクロガネモチ]</ref>。
=== 堺市指定保存樹木 === * [[クスノキ]] - 指定番号8、指定年月日：[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月15日<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/hozen/kojumeiboku.html 堺市保存樹木・樹林一覧]</ref>。 * [[イチョウ]] - 指定番号9、指定年月日：1980年（昭和55年）3月15日。 <gallery> Kuroganemochi Hochigai Shrine,Sakai.jpg|けやき通りのくろがねもち Preserved tree, houchigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキ Sign of preserved tree, houtigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識1 Sign preserved tree, houtigai shine2.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識2 </gallery>
== 祭事・行事 == 以下の祭事・行事がある。 * 粽祭（例大祭）（5月31日） - 粽（ちまき）を供える祭り<ref>神山幸雄「方違神社」（大阪春秋社『大阪春秋』第9巻第3号、p.87、1981年）</ref>。方違神社は摂津・河内・和泉のどこにも属さない場所にあるという考え方から、境内の土で作られた粽は悪い方位を祓うとされる<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.171、2008年</ref>。神功皇后が祓をしたという故事に由来する<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.192、2008年</ref>。当日は13時から神事が行われる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://hochigai-jinja.or.jp/5gatu.html |title=方違神社 粽祭り |publisher =方違神社|accessdate =2018-07-14}}</ref>。 * 初午祭（8月6日） * 月次祭（毎月1日・15日）
== 故事== [[神武天皇]]の東征時、その兄が反旗を翻した。神武天皇の夢まくらに老人が立ち、瓦を作り、飴を作って神前に備えるとどんな逆賊も平定できるとのお告げを得て、兄の一族を降参させた。その後、神后皇后が堺の浦に上陸し、三国丘に上陸した際、葦の葉に当地の埴土を包んで方災の祓をし、埴土で飴を作って神前に備え、治国平天下を祈願した。
二月の節分に飴を売る店が出て、一家が祝って食べる習慣が残る。また、方違神社の紋が[[ちまき]]であるのは、葦の葉に埴土を包んで方災よけをされた神后皇后の故事により、5月31日の例祭には[[ちまき]]を供える<ref>伝説の堺 p.40−42</ref>。
== 交通機関 == * [[南海電気鉄道|南海]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から東へ徒歩5分 * [[阪和線|JR阪和線]][[堺市駅]]より西へ徒歩10分 * [[南海バス]]方違神社前 下車すぐ * [[阪神高速15号堺線]] 堺出口より車で5分

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

{{Shrine | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}}], {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|素盞嗚尊}}, Mitsutsu no O Okami [{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Established = 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi|ja|和泉国}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

79 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:方違神社 up to rev 98320804
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=方違神社 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1 | 位置={{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神= 方違幸大神（八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気足長姫命（[[神功皇后]]）） | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年） | 本殿= | 例祭 = 5月31日 | 画像=[[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=方違神社|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''方違神社'''（ほうちがいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]にある[[神社]]。地元では「ほうちがいさん」と称されている。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。祭神は方違幸大神（かたたがえさちおおかみ）。古くから[[方違え]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 社伝では、[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年）に、勅願により物部大母呂隅足尼（もののべのおおもろすみのすくね）を茅渟の石津原に遣わせて[[スサノオ|須佐之男神]]を祀らせたとする。これが当社創建の起源という。
その後、[[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]終了後に[[忍熊皇子|忍熊王]]の叛乱に遭った際、当地に[[天神地祇]]を祀って方災除けを祈願し、合戦に勝利している。更に[[応神天皇]]が須佐之男神と天神地祇の他に三筒男神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）と神功皇后を祀り、方違大依羅神社（かたたがへおおよさみのかむつやしろ）と号し、この社を方違宮と称したとされる。
社地は[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境の三国山（現・三国ヶ丘）にあり、三[[令制国]]のいずれにも属さない地、方位のない地であるとして、古くから[[方位]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として有名であった。
[[奈良時代]]には[[行基]]が伏屋や井戸を設けて人馬往来の要衝として栄えた。[[天平]]15年（[[743年]]）には後に[[神宮寺]]となる向泉寺（現・[[大阪府立三国丘高等学校|三国丘高校]]の地）が建立されている。
[[平安時代]]には[[熊野街道]]にあったため[[熊野詣]]の人々が参詣して旅の安全を祈願した。
[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に向泉寺が兵火で焼失し、後に現・堺区市之町東に再建された。
[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）には[[東京奠都]]の折に17日間の祈祷の命を蒙った。また、[[神仏分離]]によって神宮寺の向泉寺は廃寺とされた。
[[1873年]]（明治6年）3月、[[郷社]]に列している。[[1907年]]（明治40年）2月に[[水天宮]]社を合祀し、同年10月には当社の南東にあり[[泉北郡]][[向井町|向井村]]の氏神社である向井神社（[[牛頭天王]]社）とその境内社（[[愛宕神社]]、[[神明社]]、小祠四社）を合祀して方違天王神社と改称するが、翌11月に方違神社に復号した。[[1908年]]（明治41年）12月、[[八幡社]]と武内社を合祀する。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）7月10日の堺大空襲（第6回[[大阪大空襲]]）で社殿が焼失するが、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に再建した。[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月に社殿が建て替えられた<ref>ザ・古墳群 p.104</ref>。
現在でも、転勤、結婚などでの転宅や海外旅行などの際に祈願する参拝者が多い。自分の在所から出かけていく先の方位についてのお祓いをしてもらい、清めの御砂を頂いて、自分の家の四方に撒く。
古くから方除けには当地の埴土を包んだ[[ちまき]]を奉ったことから、厄除守に御札のほか御砂、ちまきがある。
境内には三国丘碑、養蚕興業碑などがある。当社は[[反正天皇]]陵の北東部に接している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 方違幸大神［八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）］ * 合祀神 - 向井大神（大鷦鷯天皇（[[仁徳天皇]]）、去来穂別天皇（[[履中天皇]]）、瑞歯別天皇（[[反正天皇]]）、[[菟道稚郎子]]命、[[百済]][[王仁]]）、[[水天宮]]外十二柱神
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月再建。 * 幣殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 拝殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 神庫 * 祭具庫 * 社務所 * 楠木姫大神 * 大年大明神 * 鈴山大明神 * 白髪大明神 * 末次大明神 * [[八幡大神]] * [[神明社]] * 神功皇后御馬繁之松 <gallery> Hochigai-jinja haiden.jpg|方違神社の旧拝殿 2007年7月15日撮影 Houchigai jinja honden.jpg|新拝殿の斜めショット Mikunigaoka sekihi 01.jpg|三国丘石碑 Mikunigaoka sekihi 02.jpg|平木林作石碑 </gallery>
== 文化財== === 大阪府指定天然記念物 === * [[クロガネモチ|くろがねもち]] - 境内ではなく、当社の南東側、通称「けやき通り」の歩道内にある。この地はかつて向井神社の境内であった。胸高径1.1m、幹周3.5m、樹高6.8m（[[1992年]]時点）。[[1973年]]（昭和48年）3月30日、大阪府から[[天然記念物]]に指定された。[[2018年]]現在は樹勢が衰えており、鉄柱によって支えられている<ref>[http://www.tree-flower.jp/27/hochigai_jinja/kuroganemochi.htm 方違神社のクロガネモチ]</ref>。
=== 堺市指定保存樹木 === * [[クスノキ]] - 指定番号8、指定年月日：[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月15日<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/hozen/kojumeiboku.html 堺市保存樹木・樹林一覧]</ref>。 * [[イチョウ]] - 指定番号9、指定年月日：1980年（昭和55年）3月15日。 <gallery> Kuroganemochi Hochigai Shrine,Sakai.jpg|けやき通りのくろがねもち Preserved tree, houchigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキ Sign of preserved tree, houtigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識1 Sign preserved tree, houtigai shine2.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識2 </gallery>
== 祭事・行事 == 以下の祭事・行事がある。 * 粽祭（例大祭）（5月31日） - 粽（ちまき）を供える祭り<ref>神山幸雄「方違神社」（大阪春秋社『大阪春秋』第9巻第3号、p.87、1981年）</ref>。方違神社は摂津・河内・和泉のどこにも属さない場所にあるという考え方から、境内の土で作られた粽は悪い方位を祓うとされる<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.171、2008年</ref>。神功皇后が祓をしたという故事に由来する<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.192、2008年</ref>。当日は13時から神事が行われる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://hochigai-jinja.or.jp/5gatu.html |title=方違神社 粽祭り |publisher =方違神社|accessdate =2018-07-14}}</ref>。 * 初午祭（8月6日） * 月次祭（毎月1日・15日）
== 故事== [[神武天皇]]の東征時、その兄が反旗を翻した。神武天皇の夢まくらに老人が立ち、瓦を作り、飴を作って神前に備えるとどんな逆賊も平定できるとのお告げを得て、兄の一族を降参させた。その後、神后皇后が堺の浦に上陸し、三国丘に上陸した際、葦の葉に当地の埴土を包んで方災の祓をし、埴土で飴を作って神前に備え、治国平天下を祈願した。
二月の節分に飴を売る店が出て、一家が祝って食べる習慣が残る。また、方違神社の紋が[[ちまき]]であるのは、葦の葉に埴土を包んで方災よけをされた神后皇后の故事により、5月31日の例祭には[[ちまき]]を供える<ref>伝説の堺 p.40−42</ref>。
== 交通機関 == * [[南海電気鉄道|南海]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から東へ徒歩5分 * [[阪和線|JR阪和線]][[堺市駅]]より西へ徒歩10分 * [[南海バス]]方違神社前 下車すぐ * [[阪神高速15号堺線]] 堺出口より車で5分
== 出典 == {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[方違神社]] to [[Katamigae Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=方違神社 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1 | 位置={{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神= 方違幸大神（八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気足長姫命（[[神功皇后]]）） | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年） | 本殿= | 例祭 = 5月31日 | 画像=[[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=方違神社|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''方違神社'''（ほうちがいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]にある[[神社]]。地元では「ほうちがいさん」と称されている。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。祭神は方違幸大神（かたたがえさちおおかみ）。古くから[[方違え]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 社伝では、[[崇神天皇]]8年12月29日（紀元前90年）に、勅願により物部大母呂隅足尼（もののべのおおもろすみのすくね）を茅渟の石津原に遣わせて[[スサノオ|須佐之男神]]を祀らせたとする。これが当社創建の起源という。
その後、[[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]終了後に[[忍熊皇子|忍熊王]]の叛乱に遭った際、当地に[[天神地祇]]を祀って方災除けを祈願し、合戦に勝利している。更に[[応神天皇]]が須佐之男神と天神地祇の他に三筒男神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）と神功皇后を祀り、方違大依羅神社（かたたがへおおよさみのかむつやしろ）と号し、この社を方違宮と称したとされる。
社地は[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境の三国山（現・三国ヶ丘）にあり、三[[令制国]]のいずれにも属さない地、方位のない地であるとして、古くから[[方位]]、地相、[[家相]]などの方災除けの神社として有名であった。
[[奈良時代]]には[[行基]]が伏屋や井戸を設けて人馬往来の要衝として栄えた。[[天平]]15年（[[743年]]）には後に[[神宮寺]]となる向泉寺（現・[[大阪府立三国丘高等学校|三国丘高校]]の地）が建立されている。
[[平安時代]]には[[熊野街道]]にあったため[[熊野詣]]の人々が参詣して旅の安全を祈願した。
[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に向泉寺が兵火で焼失し、後に現・堺区市之町東に再建された。
[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）には[[東京奠都]]の折に17日間の祈祷の命を蒙った。また、[[神仏分離]]によって神宮寺の向泉寺は廃寺とされた。
[[1873年]]（明治6年）3月、[[郷社]]に列している。[[1907年]]（明治40年）2月に[[水天宮]]社を合祀し、同年10月には当社の南東にあり[[泉北郡]][[向井町|向井村]]の氏神社である向井神社（[[牛頭天王]]社）とその境内社（[[愛宕神社]]、[[神明社]]、小祠四社）を合祀して方違天王神社と改称するが、翌11月に方違神社に復号した。[[1908年]]（明治41年）12月、[[八幡社]]と武内社を合祀する。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）7月10日の堺大空襲（第6回[[大阪大空襲]]）で社殿が焼失するが、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に再建した。[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月に社殿が建て替えられた<ref>ザ・古墳群 p.104</ref>。
現在でも、転勤、結婚などでの転宅や海外旅行などの際に祈願する参拝者が多い。自分の在所から出かけていく先の方位についてのお祓いをしてもらい、清めの御砂を頂いて、自分の家の四方に撒く。
古くから方除けには当地の埴土を包んだ[[ちまき]]を奉ったことから、厄除守に御札のほか御砂、ちまきがある。
境内には三国丘碑、養蚕興業碑などがある。当社は[[反正天皇]]陵の北東部に接している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 方違幸大神［八十天万魂神（[[天神地祇]]）、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、三筒男大神（[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]）、息気長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）］ * 合祀神 - 向井大神（大鷦鷯天皇（[[仁徳天皇]]）、去来穂別天皇（[[履中天皇]]）、瑞歯別天皇（[[反正天皇]]）、[[菟道稚郎子]]命、[[百済]][[王仁]]）、[[水天宮]]外十二柱神
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）12月再建。 * 幣殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 拝殿 - 2017年（平成29年）12月再建。 * 神庫 * 祭具庫 * 社務所 * 楠木姫大神 * 大年大明神 * 鈴山大明神 * 白髪大明神 * 末次大明神 * [[八幡大神]] * [[神明社]] * 神功皇后御馬繁之松 <gallery> Hochigai-jinja haiden.jpg|方違神社の旧拝殿 2007年7月15日撮影 Houchigai jinja honden.jpg|新拝殿の斜めショット Mikunigaoka sekihi 01.jpg|三国丘石碑 Mikunigaoka sekihi 02.jpg|平木林作石碑 </gallery>
== 文化財== === 大阪府指定天然記念物 === * [[クロガネモチ|くろがねもち]] - 境内ではなく、当社の南東側、通称「けやき通り」の歩道内にある。この地はかつて向井神社の境内であった。胸高径1.1m、幹周3.5m、樹高6.8m（[[1992年]]時点）。[[1973年]]（昭和48年）3月30日、大阪府から[[天然記念物]]に指定された。[[2018年]]現在は樹勢が衰えており、鉄柱によって支えられている<ref>[http://www.tree-flower.jp/27/hochigai_jinja/kuroganemochi.htm 方違神社のクロガネモチ]</ref>。
=== 堺市指定保存樹木 === * [[クスノキ]] - 指定番号8、指定年月日：[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月15日<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/hozen/kojumeiboku.html 堺市保存樹木・樹林一覧]</ref>。 * [[イチョウ]] - 指定番号9、指定年月日：1980年（昭和55年）3月15日。 <gallery> Kuroganemochi Hochigai Shrine,Sakai.jpg|けやき通りのくろがねもち Preserved tree, houchigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキ Sign of preserved tree, houtigai shine1.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識1 Sign preserved tree, houtigai shine2.jpg|堺市指定保存樹木クスノキの標識2 </gallery>
== 祭事・行事 == 以下の祭事・行事がある。 * 粽祭（例大祭）（5月31日） - 粽（ちまき）を供える祭り<ref>神山幸雄「方違神社」（大阪春秋社『大阪春秋』第9巻第3号、p.87、1981年）</ref>。方違神社は摂津・河内・和泉のどこにも属さない場所にあるという考え方から、境内の土で作られた粽は悪い方位を祓うとされる<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.171、2008年</ref>。神功皇后が祓をしたという故事に由来する<ref>大阪府教育委員会『大阪府の御供物行事』、p.192、2008年</ref>。当日は13時から神事が行われる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://hochigai-jinja.or.jp/5gatu.html |title=方違神社 粽祭り |publisher =方違神社|accessdate =2018-07-14}}</ref>。 * 初午祭（8月6日） * 月次祭（毎月1日・15日）
== 故事== [[神武天皇]]の東征時、その兄が反旗を翻した。神武天皇の夢まくらに老人が立ち、瓦を作り、飴を作って神前に備えるとどんな逆賊も平定できるとのお告げを得て、兄の一族を降参させた。その後、神后皇后が堺の浦に上陸し、三国丘に上陸した際、葦の葉に当地の埴土を包んで方災の祓をし、埴土で飴を作って神前に備え、治国平天下を祈願した。
二月の節分に飴を売る店が出て、一家が祝って食べる習慣が残る。また、方違神社の紋が[[ちまき]]であるのは、葦の葉に埴土を包んで方災よけをされた神后皇后の故事により、5月31日の例祭には[[ちまき]]を供える<ref>伝説の堺 p.40−42</ref>。

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Chigi|qq=}}], [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], Mitsutsu no O Okami [[[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Established = 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Chigi|qq=}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hochigai Shrine | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|37|N|135|29|22|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Hochigai Sachi Okami (Yasotenmankonjin [{{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Chigi|qq=}}], [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], Mitsutsu no O Okami [[[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]], Ikishitanaganagahime no Mikoto [[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]]) | Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Established = 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] (December 29, 90 BCE) | Main_Facility = | Annual_Festival = May 31 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Houchigai-jinja.jpg|250px]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hochigai Shrine|float=center|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|43992121}} '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.
== History ==
According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.
Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Chigi|qq=}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.
The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
...

Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. '''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

| Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}

{{osm box|w|43992121}}
'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

{{osm box|w|43992121}}
'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]], was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

{{osm box|w|43992121}}
'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

{{osm box|w|43992121}}
'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

...

{{osm box|w|43992121}}
'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku, Sakai|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka|1=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu|1=Settsu Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi|1=Izumi Province}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.

...

{{osm box|w|43992121}}
'''Hochigai Shrine''' (方違神社, Hochigai Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|qid=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku, Sakai|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka|1=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. Locally known as "Hochigaisan," the shrine holds the status of a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deity is Hochigai Sachi Okami (かたたがえさちおおかみ). The shrine has long been revered for its power to ward off calamities related to directions, land features, and house orientations.

== History ==

According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'s reign (December 29, 90 BCE). It is said that the Emperor sent Mononobe no Ōmoro Suminomikoto to the Chinu no Ishizuhara area to enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. This event marks the origin of the shrine.

Later, during {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}'s conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, she is said to have invoked the protection of the {{ill|Tenjin Chigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} against directional calamities at this location, achieving victory. {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} is also recorded to have enshrined Susanoo, the Tenjin Chigi, the Sumiyoshi deities, and Empress Jingū at this site. This shrine became known as "Hochigai Ōyosami no Kamutsuyashiro," commonly referred to as Hochigai Shrine.

The site, located on the boundary of the former provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu|1=Settsu Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi|1=Izumi Province}}, was considered a place without directional affiliations, making it a prominent location for directional warding rituals.
